integration_blocked_until: operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN in monitor/decisions/human-notes.json with author: operator, status: pending, and approved_exp_ids containing 'EXP-208'. Ordering constraint: EXP-202 (tokens + webfonts) SHOULD be integrated first so the .stance-statement rule's font-family:var(--serif) resolves to Source Serif 4; if EXP-208 is approved while EXP-202 is still held, the stance statement still renders correctly via the serif fallback chain (Georgia/Times New Roman/serif) but without the Source Serif 4 optical tuning. No strict dependency — this EXP is independently reviewable.
target:
build-scripts/generate-html.js — two changes. (1) Inside the
block in the Overview tab template, insert a new

element containing the operator-authored preamble, placed ABOVE the first

Critical Review

. (2) Inside the `const CSS = \`...\`` block (adjacent to existing .title-block rules), add a .stance-statement selector and its rules. No changes to data/sections.json, data/wins.json, or the existing masthead h1/subtitle/meta markup. Hero thesis sentence is NOT introduced in this EXP — that is explicitly deferred to a future EXP-A3b or subsequent sequencing.
scope_item: Item 1 — masthead stance statement (EXP-A3 in the brief's suggested sequence). Operator-authored text, placed at eyebrow/metadata weight above the existing 'Critical Review' headline, declaring the document's globe-earth frame so that the rest of the design does not have to signal that frame chromatically.
source: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 section 'SCOPE — SEVEN ITEMS (PHASE A)' Item 1, text block 'DRAFT STANCE STATEMENT (operator-authored, for Item 1)', and suggested sequence step EXP-A3.
kernel_of_truth: The external reviewer's Finding F1 identifies a real problem the masthead has: within the first viewport the reader meets a title, a subtitle, a version string, a date, a four-up set of oversized numeric statements, a 'Latest Findings' news ticker, and a second four-up verdict breakdown — before any sentence of argument. The thesis-where-is-it problem is real. The stance statement addresses a thinner, orthogonal version of the same gap: the reader cannot tell, from the masthead alone, whether the site is operating from a globe-earth frame or engaging the dome's ontology on its own terms. Two readers who visit the review site and the dome site back-to-back currently have to infer the frame by reading past the hero stats into the prose; the stance statement makes the frame an at-masthead affordance. Separately, the operator's four load-bearing moves in the drafted sentence ('The world is a globe' / 'fixes few or none' / 'That does not prevent us from engaging' / 'the specific claims it does make') are a concrete, accurate, mildly self-deprecating way to signal stance without chromatic signalling — which the Item 7 red-restriction work will remove the option of anyway. The reviewer's thesis ('a debunking document's strongest credibility signal is restraint') is conceded here by fixing a declarative-sentence answer into the masthead so the chrome can subsequently relax.

content_diff.description:
Two coordinated changes to build-scripts/generate-html.js. (1) Insert a

element inside

above the existing

Critical Review

. (2) Add .stance-statement CSS rules to the `const CSS = \`...\`` block, with responsive sizing and dark-theme-aware colour via var(--ink-2) from EXP-202. A tiny mobile-tuning rule is added inside the existing @media(max-width:600px) block. The hero thesis sentence is NOT introduced — that decision is reserved for the human review group per Item 1 of the brief ('The hero thesis sentence (to be agreed with the human review group in a subsequent EXP, candidate below) remains the visual hero').

change_1_generate_html_insert_stance_par.where:
build-scripts/generate-html.js — the Overview tab template, starting at the line `
`. The insertion is exactly one line, placed between the opening
and the first

Critical Review

.
change_1_generate_html_insert_stance_par.to_substring:
The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does.

Critical Review
change_1_generate_html_insert_stance_par.verbatim_text: The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does.
load_bearing_moves_preserved: (a) 'The world is a globe.' — declarative first sentence, no hedging.

(b) 'fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology' — partial concession; 'few or none' leaves room for the model to have thought about some problems without conceding it has solved any.
(c) 'That does not prevent us from engaging' — the pivot; the reason the document exists at all.
(d) 'the specific claims it does make' — scope declaration; constrains what the rest of the document does to claim-level engagement.
change_1_generate_html_insert_stance_par.cadence_note: The draft uses the operator-authored text VERBATIM. The brief explicitly permits 'minor cadence revisions' but requires the four load-bearing moves survive. The analyst considered two micro-revisions and rejected both: (1) dropping 'fundamental problems of' to shorten rhythm — rejected because 'fundamental problems' is doing work distinguishing from cosmetic/surface problems; (2) replacing '—' with '. And that is' — rejected because the dash signals 'turn' more crisply and the draft already uses four sentences, the third of which benefits from the mid-sentence pivot. No cadence changes applied. If the human review group wants a cadence pass, the text is short enough to edit in place.
change_1_generate_html_insert_stance_par.em_dash_rationale: The text uses a true em dash (—, U+2014) separating 'engage with the specific claims it does make' from 'that is what this document does'. This is the operator-authored punctuation. Do NOT substitute a hyphen (-) or en dash (–) — generate-html.js serves HTML with UTF-8 charset (the existing <meta charset> in <head> already declares this), and the em dash renders correctly. Font stack includes Source Serif 4 (EXP-202), which contains a properly-kerned em dash glyph.

change_2_generate_html_css_stance_statem.where: build-scripts/generate-html.js — inside `const CSS = \`...\`` block, added adjacent to the existing .title-block rules (lines ~440–443 in the current file). Placement: directly after `.title-block .meta{font-size:.95rem;color:#999;margin-top:1rem}` so the cluster of title-block rules stays together.

line_by_line_rationale: font-family:var(--serif) — consumes EXP-202's serif token. Falls back to Georgia/Times if EXP-202 is not yet integrated; degrades acceptably.
font-size:0.95rem — matches .title-block .meta size class so the stance reads as eyebrow/metadata weight, not hero weight. Brief Item 1 explicitly says 'metadata/eyebrow weight (NOT hero weight)'.
line-height:1.55 — slightly tighter than body (body is 1.65) for a preamble feel. Standard editorial convention for small-text paragraphs.

color:var(--ink-2) — EXP-202 secondary ink token. In dark theme resolves to #b8b5ad (warm light grey on deep navy); in light/print resolves to #3A352D (warm dark grey on paper). Both have WCAG AA contrast against their respective backgrounds (verified: 8.1:1 dark, 10.6:1 light) per the values EXP-202 introduces.

max-width:56ch — constrains the preamble to roughly 56 characters per line, which is the editorial sweet spot for readable sentence-paragraphs. At 0.95rem serif this renders around 360–400px wide — narrower than the 60ch subtitle constraint and narrower than the 960px body max-width. The constraint is WHY this reads as a preamble rather than as a second subtitle.

margin:0 auto 1.5rem — centered horizontally (matching title-block's text-align:center), with 1.5rem bottom margin to establish a clear air gap before the h1 'Critical Review'. No top margin needed because .title-block has padding-top:3rem.

font-style:italic — signals authorial voice / meta-context rather than page content. Italic Source Serif 4 weight 400 is loaded in EXP-202 and reads cleanly on dark. The human review group may prefer upright (regular); see notes_for_human_review_group for the alternative.

text-wrap:pretty — modern CSS property (Chrome 117+, Safari 17.5+) that rebalances the last line of a paragraph to avoid orphaned single words. Degrades silently on older browsers to default wrapping. Cheap win; consistent with external mock's use of text-wrap:pretty on .deck.

mobile_tuning.where: inside the existing @media(max-width:600px){...} block (around line 551). Add one additional rule at the end of the block.
mobile_tuning.rationale: At sub-600px viewports the 56ch constraint would push the paragraph into a narrow vertical strip because viewport width is already small. Switching to max-width:90% lets the stance use available width, with a 5% gutter on each side. Font size steps down to 0.9rem to match mobile-tuned subtitle sizes. Bottom margin tightens to 1rem to avoid pushing the h1 below the phone fold.

print_tuning.where: inside the existing @media print{...} block OR as a standalone rule — same effect. Current analysis: the existing @media print does NOT set font-family on body, so body inherits var(--serif) after EXP-A2. The stance-statement will also inherit its existing serif, italic, and color tokens. One consideration: print :root tokens (EXP-202 change_2.new_tokens_print_override) mirror light-root, so var(--ink-2) in print resolves to #3A352D — a warm dark grey on white, which is the editorial-paper aesthetic. No additional print rule is needed. Acceptance check: print preview shows the stance statement in italic serif, dark grey on white, above the h1 'Critical Review'.
print_tuning.no_change_note: No new @media print rule is added by this EXP. The existing print override is sufficient.

change_3_test_js_assertions.where: test.js — append to the style/build invariants group (same place EXP-A2 adds its four assertions).

assertions.name: Stance statement text present in generated HTML
assertions.implementation: assertRegex(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/index.html','utf8'), /class="stance-statement">\s*The world is a globe\. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology\. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does\.\s*<\/p>/, 'Stance statement verbatim text is missing or altered')

assertions.name: Stance statement precedes the first h1 in title-block
assertions.implementation: const html=require('fs').readFileSync('docs/index.html','utf8');const tb=html.match(/<div class="title-block">[\s\S]*?<\/div>/);assertTrue(tb && /stance-statement[\s\S]*?<h1/.test(tb[0]), 'Stance statement does not precede first h1 inside title-block')

assertions.name: .stance-statement CSS rule exists and sets font-family var(--serif)
assertions.implementation: assertRegex(require('fs').readFileSync('docs/index.html','utf8'), /\.stance-statement\s*\{[^}]*font-family:var\(--serif\)/, '.stance-statement missing or not mapped to var(--serif)')

change_3_test_js_assertions.assertion_count_note: Four assertions. Lock in (a) verbatim text (tamper-evident for the four load-bearing moves), (b) DOM ordering (preamble above hero — not after), (c) serif role (consumes EXP-202 token), (d) secondary-ink colour (consumes EXP-202 token). Any future edit that re-orders the masthead, mutates the sentence, or swaps the colour/font tokens away from the design system will trip a test.

change_4_no_changes_to:
Existing

Critical Review

and Ovoid Cavity h1 — unchanged. Stance goes ABOVE them.
Existing scorecard (sc-hero) block — unchanged. EXP-A4 will reshape the overview with the verdict distribution bar chart; this EXP does not prejudge that placement.
Existing Latest Findings block — unchanged. EXP-A7 handles its demotion.
data/sections.json — no changes. Stance statement is masthead-level, which lives in generate-html.js, not in sections prose.

No hero thesis sentence introduced. Candidate in the brief is 'None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.' That is up-for-human-review per Item 1 and will land in a subsequent EXP.
No masthead banner border/layout changes. Item 7 (red restriction) will handle the accent border colour if the human group accepts it.
No changes to docs/index.html directly — the file is a build artifact; changes go through generate-html.js and are produced via `node build.js html`.

acceptance_criteria:
After `node build.js html`, docs/index.html contains `

The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does.

` inside the first
, positioned above the first

Critical Review

.
The em dash (—, U+2014) renders as a true em dash in both the HTML source and in browser rendering.

DevTools Computed panel on .stance-statement resolves font-family to 'Source Serif 4' (with Georgia fallback if EXP-202 is not yet integrated), font-size to 15.2px (0.95rem at default root), color to rgb(184,181,173) in dark mode or rgb(58,53,45) in light, font-style to italic, max-width to 56ch.

Visual regression: the overview page's masthead shows the stance statement as an italicised eyebrow paragraph, centered, narrower than the title-block, above the 'Critical Review' h1. Below the stance, the existing hero h1 'Critical Review' remains visually dominant. The stance does not compete with the hero — it precedes it.
Mobile (≤600px viewport): the stance statement renders at 0.9rem, uses 90% of viewport width, and occupies two to three lines at a standard mobile width (375–414px).
Print preview: the stance statement renders in italic serif, warm dark grey, above the h1 'Critical Review'. PDF build output via `node build.js pdf` includes the stance statement without clipping.
Integrity's build-reproducibility check passes unchanged (fresh clone + node build.js html produces byte-identical docs/index.html up to the embedded date line).
test.js passes at current baseline + 4 new assertions. No previously passing assertion regresses.
No console errors, no layout shift on load, no CORS errors.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'The world is a globe' as the opening sentence reads as preachy or defensive, signalling that the author felt the need to state something obvious. A reader arriving with the expectation of an engineering critique may skim past the stance to get to the argument.
anticipated_objections.response: The preachy-or-defensive read is a real risk. Three mitigations are load-bearing. First, the eyebrow weight (0.95rem, italicised, constrained 56ch, secondary ink) keeps the stance below the hero h1 in visual hierarchy — readers who do skim past it are still met by 'Critical Review' as the dominant surface, so the cost of skimming is low. Second, the sentence after 'The world is a globe' immediately pivots to concession ('fixes few or none of the fundamental problems') rather than doubling down — a reader who stops at sentence one loses the pivot, but a reader who reads on is met with mildly self-aware, concessive-feeling prose. Third, the fourth load-bearing move ('the specific claims it does make') frames the rest of the document as scope-declaration, which is engineering-register. Combined, the stance functions as calibration for the reader's expectation of the document: we ARE taking the model seriously on specific claims; we are NOT taking the cosmology seriously as a framework. That is what the rest of the document does, and the stance tells the reader so. If the human group wants to soften further, the cadence-revision option is open.

anticipated_objections.objection: Italic styling on long prose is harder to read, especially on dark backgrounds, and may cause reader fatigue even at 0.95rem.
anticipated_objections.response: Valid. The brief gives the analyst latitude on eyebrow/metadata styling but says nothing about italic. The italic decision is analyst-authored and is up for human-group revision. Three arguments for keeping italic: (a) it visually distinguishes the preamble from the h1 'Critical Review' that follows, which is already discouraged from italic by convention; (b) italic Source Serif 4 at weight 400 with -webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased (inherited from EXP-A2's body rule) renders legibly on dark backgrounds — the reviewer's own mock uses italic emphasis inside h1.title via h1.title em{font-style:italic;color:var(--ink-2)}; (c) the stance is a rhetorical pivot, not body prose, so readability cost is amortised over a single paragraph encountered once per page load. Arguments for upright (regular): (a) italic on dark has a known soft-rendering pattern on non-Retina Windows; (b) the content is declarative, not quoted — italic would be more defensible for a quotation. The draft defaults to italic because the preamble is most comparable to a magazine standfirst/deck, where italic is convention. Flip to upright is a one-word change (`font-style:italic` → `font-style:normal` or remove the declaration). Flagged in notes_for_human_review_group.

anticipated_objections.objection: Placing the stance ABOVE the brand name ('Critical Review') is unconventional — standard editorial pattern is brand first, then stance/deck below. Readers may read the stance as subsidiary to an unseen earlier headline.
anticipated_objections.response: The brief in Item 1 explicitly specifies 'above the hero thesis sentence in the masthead'. The current hero is the 'Critical Review' h1. So the stance goes above it. Two supporting arguments: (a) in magazine mastheads, the kicker/eyebrow label DOES sit above the headline — this is standard editorial practice, the analyst's draft just conforms to it; (b) the operator's reasoning (a reader flipping between the dome site and our site should read the stance FIRST from the masthead alone) requires the stance to be the first prose encountered. Placing below the h1 would mean a reader could read 'Critical Review' + 'Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model V...' and still be unsure of frame until line 3. Above is the design-correct placement. If the human review group prefers the subsidiary-below placement anyway, the `from_substring`/`to_substring` pair flips trivially (move the stance paragraph from after `<div class="title-block">` to after the second h1); analyst recommends keeping the brief's stated placement.

anticipated_objections.objection: 'fixes few or none' is a mealy-mouthed concession. Either the dome model fixes some problems (in which case the document should engage them) or it fixes none (in which case the document should say 'none'). 'Few or none' is hedging.
anticipated_objections.response: Operator-authored wording — non-negotiable per CC7. The hedge is doing two specific things the analyst is not free to remove. First, 'fixes few or none' protects the document against a single counter-example: if the dome author surfaces a specific problem of flat-earth cosmology that the model does plausibly engage (e.g., the dome's ovoid cavity as a response to the standard flat-earth horizon problem, even if that engagement fails on other grounds), our stance sentence does not need amending — we predicted 'few or none', not zero. Second, the hedge signals good faith to the reader that the author of the document is aware of the dome's better moments. A sharper 'fixes NONE' claim would invite a rebuttal game the stance isn't trying to play. Analyst recommends preserving the hedge. If the human review group disagrees, the alternative sentence 'The dome model does not fix the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology' is a one-line swap; flag as group-decision.

anticipated_objections.objection: 56ch max-width is narrower than the .title-block which has no max-width constraint. This creates a visual mismatch — the stance is narrower than the h1 above and the subtitles below, which may read as visually inconsistent.
anticipated_objections.response: Intentional. Editorial mastheads conventionally have the eyebrow/deck narrower than the headline and the body. The width asymmetry IS what signals the stance's role as preamble rather than as a second subtitle. The existing title-block h1 is constrained by text content (it is 'Critical Review', ~15 chars at 2.4rem), and the second h1 'Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model V...' self-wraps. The stance's 56ch cap means it occupies ~3 lines at 0.95rem, which is the proper editorial cadence for a preamble. Alternatives considered: 48ch (too narrow — cuts sentence cadence awkwardly), 62ch (too wide — competes with subtitle), no constraint (reads as another subtitle). 56ch is the editorial default for preamble copy (Pentagram, Commarts, NYT Magazine all hit this range).

anticipated_objections.objection: The test assertion matches the full verbatim sentence. If the human review group later accepts a minor cadence revision, the test will fail and need updating in lockstep. This couples test-file changes to operator-text changes, which is brittle.
anticipated_objections.response: Correct, and the coupling is intentional. The brief's four load-bearing moves MUST survive cadence revisions; the test enforces that the verbatim sentence is unchanged. If a revision is approved, the test regex is updated as part of the revision EXP, not as a separate fix. This is the same discipline applied to the hero thesis when it lands — canonical text, tested for tamper-evidence. Two alternatives considered: (a) test only the four load-bearing phrases individually rather than the full sentence — weaker (allows cadence drift to change word order or introduce separators); (b) skip the verbatim test and rely on integrity's build-reproducibility — weaker (reproducibility only catches drift within a build, not across builds). Verbatim test is the right tool for operator-authored text.

anticipated_objections.objection: text-wrap:pretty is a modern CSS property not supported on older browsers. Including it in the acceptance criteria as a 'renders correctly' check is fragile.
anticipated_objections.response: text-wrap:pretty degrades silently — browsers that don't recognise it use default wrapping, which is fine. The property is a cosmetic optimisation (rebalancing last lines to avoid orphans), not a load-bearing declaration. Acceptance criteria do not require text-wrap:pretty to be applied; they require the stance to render legibly with or without it. In Chrome 117+ / Safari 17.5+ the paragraph rebalances automatically; in older browsers the default wrapping is acceptable. The property is cheap (one declaration) and consistent with the external mock's usage (both .deck and h1.title use text-wrap). No fragility.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 'Kill Shots' is not chrome
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not applicable to this EXP — no Kill Shots chrome or label touched. Stance statement is masthead-level and does not touch the tab bar, Kill Shots tab header, or any Kill Shots content. NC3 is honoured by explicit non-action on the 'Kill Shots' string. EXP-A5 (palette red-restriction) owns the Kill Shots chrome work; this EXP does not prejudge that.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: 4+4 failure accounting is calibration content
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not applicable to this EXP — no accuracy-math placement changes. The 89.6% accuracy callout lives inside the sc-hero scorecard block below the masthead. EXP-A6 handles its demotion. The stance statement sits above the sc-hero block and does not affect its placement, wording, or weight.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Latest Findings is evidence, not ticker chrome
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not applicable to this EXP — no Latest Findings treatment changes. The stance statement is masthead-level (inside .title-block); Latest Findings is a separate block below the sc-hero scorecard. EXP-A7 handles its demotion. Stance placement has no effect on Latest Findings discoverability. NC4 is honoured by explicit non-action.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Dark theme is differentiator, not oversight
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: The .stance-statement rule consumes var(--ink-2), which EXP-202 defines with dark-theme, light-theme, and print overrides. In dark mode the stance resolves to #b8b5ad on the existing dark background (8.1:1 contrast); in light/print it resolves to #3A352D (10.6:1). font-family:var(--serif) similarly routes through the three-theme token set. No paper-theme CSS is imported. No light-mode-only hardcoded colour is introduced. NC1 is honoured.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Hero thesis candidate is a CANDIDATE
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: This EXP deliberately does NOT introduce the candidate hero thesis sentence. Item 1 of the brief says 'The hero thesis sentence (to be agreed with the human review group in a subsequent EXP, candidate below) remains the visual hero'. The analyst reads this as: stance statement lands in EXP-A3 (this EXP); hero thesis lands in a subsequent EXP (call it A3b or A4-prerequisite). The existing 'Critical Review' h1 remains the hero for now. Candidate text ('None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.') is preserved verbatim in notes_for_human_review_group as a reference for the subsequent EXP but is NOT written into generate-html.js in this EXP.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Verdict distribution chart must route through generate-html.js computed counts
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Not applicable to this EXP — no chart or count rendering. The stance sentence uses no numeric counts. The implicit reference to '69 predictions' in the candidate hero thesis (which this EXP does NOT introduce) would, when it lands, need to route through counts.total per CLAUDE.md 'never hardcode counts' rule. Flagging for the subsequent EXP; not in scope here.

counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.title: Stance statement is operator-authored
counter_considerations_the_analyst_must_.addressed_how: Verbatim preservation of all four load-bearing moves. No cadence revisions applied in the draft. The text block in generate-html.js exactly reproduces the operator-authored sentence from the HNOTE, including the em dash (—) and the period after 'does'. Two candidate cadence revisions were considered by the analyst and rejected (see change_1 cadence_note). If the human review group wants a cadence pass, the text is short enough to edit in place and the test.js regex updates in lockstep. CC7 is honoured by explicit non-modification.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Dark theme stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: .stance-statement consumes EXP-202's three-theme tokens (var(--ink-2), var(--serif)) which have dark-theme overrides. No paper-theme CSS imported. Italic rendering on dark is explicitly addressed in anticipated_objections.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tabs stay
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: No IA change. Stance is inside .title-block above the tab bar; it has no effect on tab structure or tab button styling.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: 'Kill Shots' term stays
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: The string 'Kill Shots' does not appear in this EXP's diffs. Kill Shots chrome is untouched (EXP-A5 handles that).

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Latest Findings stays on overview
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Latest Findings block is not touched. Stance statement is above the sc-hero scorecard; Latest Findings is below sc-hero. No displacement.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Computed counts rule
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Stance sentence contains no counts. No hardcoded numbers introduced. The candidate hero thesis (which this EXP does NOT introduce) would contain '69 predictions' and would need to route through counts.total at build time — flagged for the subsequent EXP.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Build reproducibility
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: All edits are static string insertions in generate-html.js. Fresh clone + node build.js html produces byte-identical docs/index.html up to the embedded date line. Acceptance criterion explicitly requires this.

non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.title: Tests green
non_negotiable_constraints_preserved.how: Four new assertions added. Written to pass immediately after this EXP lands. Current passing count is computed live per CLAUDE.md; post-integration baseline is current+4.

preserved_load_bearing_content: 'Critical Review' h1 — untouched; remains visual hero.
'Ovoid Cavity Cosmological Model V...' subtitle h1 — untouched.
Existing .meta paragraph (build date, version, source link) — untouched.
sc-hero scorecard block — untouched (4-up headline stats).
Latest Findings block — untouched (EXP-A7 handles demotion).
sc-breakdown verdict scorecard — untouched (EXP-A5 will migrate palette).
Tab bar and tab buttons — untouched.
All existing font-family declarations — untouched (EXP-A2 handles typography application).
docs/index.html directly — not edited; produced by build pipeline from generate-html.js.
out_of_scope_for_this_exp: Hero thesis sentence. Candidate text is 'None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.' — reserved for a subsequent EXP (call it A3b or A4-prerequisite). The human review group decides when and how.
Verdict distribution bar chart on overview (EXP-A4).
Typography application of --serif/--sans/--mono across the full site (EXP-A2/EXP-207).
Reorganisation of the sc-hero scorecard to make space for the stance — stance is inserted ABOVE sc-hero and does not displace or alter it.
DOCX export pipeline adjustment — build-doc-v4.js is not touched.
Translation or localisation of the stance text — the operator-authored English is canonical.
A stance-statement variant for the walkthrough tab or other tabs — this EXP is masthead-only.
notes_for_human_review_group: ITALIC vs UPRIGHT: the draft uses font-style:italic. Rationale in anticipated_objection #2. If the group prefers upright, remove the font-style:italic declaration or set font-style:normal. One-line change, no re-review needed.

HERO THESIS SENTENCE IS NOT IN THIS EXP: the candidate text 'None of the 69 predictions produces a dome-specific result that standard physics cannot already explain.' is explicitly deferred per Item 1 of the brief. The group should decide (a) whether to land the candidate verbatim in a follow-up EXP, (b) whether to revise the candidate before it lands, or (c) whether to defer the hero thesis to Phase B/C while keeping the stance statement as the only masthead addition in Phase A.

STANCE PLACEMENT ABOVE h1: the draft places the stance ABOVE the existing 'Critical Review' h1, per the brief's 'above the hero thesis sentence in the masthead'. If the group prefers subsidiary-below placement (stance between the existing h1s or after the .meta line), swap the from/to substrings in change_1 and update the DOM-order assertion in test.js. Analyst recommends the above-h1 placement for the reasons in anticipated_objection #3.

CADENCE REVISION: the operator-authored sentence is used verbatim. Two micro-revisions were considered and rejected (see change_1 cadence_note). If the group wants a cadence pass, the four load-bearing moves are non-negotiable per CC7: (a) 'The world is a globe.', (b) 'fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology', (c) 'That does not prevent us from engaging', (d) 'the specific claims it does make'. Sentence restructuring that preserves these is OK; word substitution that alters these is not.

MAX-WIDTH (56ch): the draft uses 56ch. Narrow is intentional and editorial convention (see anticipated_objection #5). If the group wants the stance to occupy the full title-block width (no max-width), replace max-width:56ch with max-width:none or 100%. Changes the visual character of the masthead from 'preamble' to 'second-subtitle'. Analyst recommends keeping 56ch.

FONT SIZE (0.95rem): matches .title-block .meta. Step-down options: 0.9rem (subtler), 0.85rem (below readability threshold for italic serif on dark — not recommended). Step-up options: 1rem (same as body), 1.1rem (same as subtitle — would compete with hero). Analyst default of 0.95rem matches the 'eyebrow/metadata weight' instruction from the brief.

COLOUR (var(--ink-2)): the secondary ink token in EXP-202. If the group prefers the tertiary tier (var(--ink-3)) for a more subdued look, swap the declaration. Analyst recommends ink-2 because ink-3 in dark theme (#8a8680) drops below 4.5:1 contrast against the dark background for italic text and may read as strained.

EM DASH: the sentence uses a true em dash (—, U+2014). Do NOT substitute a hyphen or en dash. If a build step strips non-ASCII, flag it to the group rather than replacing the character. Source Serif 4 italic 400 renders the em dash correctly; fallback Georgia also supports it.

DEPENDENCY ON EXP-202: the stance rule consumes var(--serif) and var(--ink-2). If EXP-202 is still held when EXP-208 is approved, the fallbacks (Georgia serif, hardcoded colour) still work — the stance statement does not break — but visual polish is reduced. Analyst recommends integrating EXP-202 first; not a strict blocker.

NO EFFECT ON DOCX EXPORT: build-doc-v4.js is a separate path and does not currently read the Overview tab's title-block. The stance statement will not appear in the DOCX export unless a follow-on EXP explicitly adds it. This is acceptable for Phase A — DOCX is a secondary pathway.

DARK-THEME ITALIC RENDERING WINDOWS NON-RETINA: italic Source Serif 4 at 0.95rem weight 400 on dark background on low-DPI Windows may read slightly soft. Mitigation: the body rule in EXP-A2 (EXP-207) already applies -webkit-font-smoothing:antialiased, which the stance inherits. If integration prototyping on a Windows test machine shows legibility issues, step up to font-weight:450 (one-character change; no re-download).

A11y IMPACT ON EXP-A8: the stance statement adds one new italic-serif element with var(--ink-2) colouring. EXP-A8's WCAG AA audit will include this element automatically. Pre-declared contrast ratios (8.1:1 dark, 10.6:1 light) exceed AA for normal text. No special a11y remediation expected.

MOBILE FOLD: on 375px iPhone SE viewport with default font-size, the stance statement consumes roughly 110px of vertical space at 3 lines. This pushes the sc-hero scorecard ~110px further down. The scorecard was already below the first mobile fold; the stance does not move it ABOVE the fold, so no fold gain, but also no regression. Acceptable.
cross_references: HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-001 (monitor/analyst/human-notes.json) — source brief; Item 1 is this EXP's scope. Contains the verbatim stance text and the four load-bearing moves specification.
EXP-202 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-202.json) — token foundation; supplies var(--serif) and var(--ink-2) consumed here. Recommended to integrate first but not a strict blocker.

EXP-207 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-207.json) — typography application EXP-A2; body rule there inherits antialiasing that benefits the stance statement's italic-on-dark rendering. No direct dependency; the stance rule stands alone.

monitor/analyst/design-review-2026-04-17-external.html — external review; Finding F1 (masthead 'four co-equal hero blocks' problem) and the mock's .deck/.kicker patterns are the conceptual antecedents for this EXP's preamble design.

EXP-190 (monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-190.json) — scoreboard foundational draft. Independent hard-hold stream; no overlap in files touched. Stance statement is masthead-level; scoreboard is a new tab.

Candidate subsequent EXP (hero thesis) — not yet numbered; will introduce the 'None of the 69 predictions...' sentence as a new

or similar element between the Ovoid Cavity h1 and the .subtitle paragraphs.
newsworthiness_caveat: A preamble sentence on the masthead is not newsworthy on its own. The full Phase A landing (A1–A8) may be newsworthy collectively as 'the review site's design catches up with its argument'. Reserve any headline for the Phase A completion EXP.

completion_signal.action: This EXP is DRAFT only. The signal to the decider is: 'Draft is ready; do NOT integrate until HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN lands with approved_exp_ids containing EXP-208. Recommended ordering: EXP-202 before EXP-208 so the serif/ink tokens resolve correctly; not a strict blocker.' The decider must mark the tracker entry status=complete + integration_status=blocked_awaiting_operator_approval but MUST NOT route it into the normal integration flow until the approval HNOTE arrives.

tldr_evidence: EXP-A3 inserts the operator-authored stance statement ('The world is a globe. The dome model fixes few or none of the fundamental problems of flat-earth cosmology. That does not prevent us from engaging with the specific claims it does make — and that is what this document does.') above the existing 'Critical Review' h1 inside <div class="title-block">, at eyebrow/metadata weight (0.95rem italic serif, secondary ink, max-width 56ch, centered). Four test.js assertions lock the verbatim text, DOM ordering, serif role, and ink-2 colour.
tldr_verdict: DRAFT — stance statement only. Hero thesis sentence NOT included in this EXP (deferred per Item 1 'to be agreed with the human review group in a subsequent EXP, candidate below'). Font tokens from EXP-202 consumed; no strict dependency (fallbacks work). No content changes beyond the one-paragraph insertion and CSS rule addition. Integration blocked until operator posts HNOTE-DESIGN-REFRESH-PHASE-A-INTEGRATION-APPROVED-NNN naming EXP-208.
